new_beliefs.claim: Agent session memory is an ephemeral cache, never a store: project and work state is banned from it - the graph owns obligations, the repos own records, CLAUDE.md compiles the bootstrap. At session end, promote anything load-bearing through the write flow, then prune; what survives between sessions is at most a thin operator-preferences layer, and only until a private overlay collection gives preferences a graph home. The a386 doctrine applied to the agent's own notebook: a cache whose freshness is procedural embeds the staleness it was meant to solve.

evidence.detail: Proposed by the operator after two same-week incidents of memory rot under multi-session concurrency (the a367 stale-todo authoring and the bootstrap handoff failure, postscripts 4 and 6). Viable now because the week's promotions emptied memory of load: a504-a506, a497, a508, and the desk made every formerly memory-resident fact graph-resident. Applied immediately: the session's memory files were pruned to a single index line pointing at the graph.
